facts.place: Slonim, Grodno Gubernia, Russian Empire (now Belarus)
facts.place_note: Likely from village of Golynka/Holinka (Yid: Holinke), Slonim District — confirmed by manifest last-residence 'Holenko'
facts.source: GEDCOM export (Geni.com); 1941 US District Court naturalization gives 14 Mar 1884 / Grodna Poland; 1917 County naturalization gives 11 Mar 1883 / Slonin Russia

facts.note: Six-point confirmed match from Statue of Liberty Arrival Details tab: (1) Name Boruch, (2) ship Pennsylvania, (3) 2 Jan 1904, (4) Hamburg departure, (5) last-residence Holenko (=Golynka/Holinka, Slonim District, Grodno Province), (6) line 0017 confirmed. Marital status 'Single' at arrival — confirms Beckie Marcus immigrated separately and later. Indexed year of birth 1886 is inconsistent with naturalization forms (1883/1884) — likely an understatement at time of entry. Traveling companions from same village Holenko: Naftoly Gasner, Abram Gordin, Meyer Chananowicz.
facts.source: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island Foundation Arrival Details (statueofliberty.org/arrival-details/?id=JFB6-923); NARA microfilm T715, roll 424, image 94, line 17

details.note: Certificate surname 'Honkofsky' is a phonetic variant of 'Hynkavsky' (the original Slavic surname). Groom's father 'Michel Honkofsky' = Michael Hiner (GEDCOM I6000000043715363867) CONFIRMED. Groom's mother 'Goldy Litkofsky' = Goldie Litcofsky (GEDCOM I6000000043715501935) CONFIRMED.

research_notes.note: Birth date discrepancy: 1917 nat. declaration says 11 Mar 1883 (Slonin Russia); 1941 nat. declaration says 14 Mar 1884 (Grodna Poland); GEDCOM (from Geni.com) says 15 Mar 1884. The 1884 dates are more consistent. The 1883 date may reflect rounding or memory error at time of filing.

ESTABLISHED SHIP MANIFEST: Boruch Yankowsky, SS Pennsylvania, Hamburg→New York, 2 Jan 1904, Line 0017, Image T715-0424-94, Passenger ID JFB6-923. Confirmed via Statue of Liberty Arrival Details tab (6-point match): (1) Hebrew name Boruch, (2) ship Pennsylvania, (3) 2 Jan 1904 arrival, (4) Hamburg departure, (5) last-residence Holenko = Golynka/Holinka (Yiddish: Holinke), Slonim District, Grodno Province (confirmed via JewishGen Communities Database), (6) line number 0017 explicitly confirmed in index. Manifest also shows: Marital Status = Single (confirms Beckie Marcus immigrated separately later), Age at Arrival = 18, Indexed year of birth = 1886 (inconsistent with nat. forms 1883/1884 — likely understated at entry). Traveling companions from same village Holenko: Naftoly Gasner, Abram Gordin, Meyer Chananowicz — consistent with chain-migration from small shtetl.

Surname evolution: Hynkavsky (pre-immigration) → various anglicizations on arrival. Manifest indexed surname 'Yankowsky' is a plausible transcription/phonetic variant (H dropped, yn→an, -sky suffix retained). FamilySearch full-text search found no results for Hynkavsky/Hinkofsky/Hinkavsky variants — surname was likely very rare or not indexed.
1940 Census found via 'Similar Records' panel on 1930 Census entry. Indexed as 'Benjamin Hegner' — indexer misread 'Hyner' as 'Hegner'. Wife Becky, sons Sidney and Irving. Brooklyn AD-23.

NYC Marriage Certificate M-K-1907-0005761 CONFIRMS parents: groom's father = 'Michel Honkofsky' (= Michael Hiner, GEDCOM I6000000043715363867) ✓; groom's mother = 'Goldy Litkofsky' (= Goldie Litcofsky, GEDCOM I6000000043715501935) ✓. Certificate surname 'Honkofsky' is a phonetic variant of 'Hynkavsky' — consistent with the ship manifest indexing 'Yankowsky' (same underlying Slavic name, different transcription). Both the GGG marriage index and the Municipal Archives confirm groom = 'Bennie Honkofsky', age 22, carpenter, birthplace Russia, resident 369 Sackman St., Brooklyn — all consistent with known facts. Also note: the name 'Sidney' for a son in the 1940 census could potentially reflect naming after bride's father — if the father's name on the marriage certificate reads 'Leidny' (possibly 'Leiby' or another form), a son named 'Sidney' could be an Americanization. Speculative but worth noting.
